# Risk & Uncertainty in LLM-Assisted Estimates
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

> Dimension 8 of 8 | See [../citations.md](../citations.md) for full source details

## Key Finding

LLM-assisted development introduces nine risk categories not present in traditional estimation. The most dangerous is "false confidence" -- developers believe AI helps 20-24% while experiencing a 19% slowdown [3]. The cone of uncertainty widens initially and may re-widen late in projects due to accumulated AI-generated technical debt.

---

## Nine AI-Specific Risk Categories

### 1. Hallucination Risk
- 19.7% of AI-recommended packages are fabricated [10]
- Commercial models: ~5.2% hallucination rate; open-source: ~21.7% [10]
- 58% of hallucinated packages repeat across runs, enabling "slopsquatting" supply chain attacks [10]
- RAG-based mitigation shows "consistent effectiveness" [12]

### 2. False Confidence / Comprehension Debt
- Developers using AI scored 17% lower on comprehension quizzes (50% vs 67%) [20]
- 39-44 pp gap between perceived and actual productivity [3]
- AI code is "syntactically clean, often well-formatted, and superficially correct" [43]
- One team stopped all development for 3 weeks because nobody understood their AI-generated codebase [43]

### 3. Non-Determinism Risk [79]
- Temperature=0 doesn't guarantee identical output (GPU floating-point parallelism)
- "Breaks traditional software testing and QA paradigms"
- Same prompt may produce different code across runs
- Estimation becomes inherently probabilistic

### 4. Context Window / Task-Size Risk [58]
- Performance degrades to 60-70% scores at long contexts
- Performance degrades silently past threshold -- no error signal
- Individual file analysis: 32K-128K tokens; full repo: 1M-10M tokens
- "Lost in the middle" effect: LLMs weight beginning and end more heavily

### 5. Capability Overestimation [80]
- Agents attempt tasks beyond abilities "with confidence that masks limitations"
- No reliable way to predict in advance whether a task is "AI-suitable"
- HumanEval: 90-96% (near-saturated); SWE-bench Verified: average 62.2% [78]
- 20-31 pp drop from HumanEval to HumanEvalNext [78]

### 6. Verification Overhead
- 59% rate review effort as "moderate" or "substantial" [28]
- GitHub Copilot suggestion acceptance rate: ~27-30% (GitHub telemetry, widely reported)
- 38% say reviewing AI code harder than human code [28]
- Senior devs: 4.3 min per suggestion vs. 1.2 min for juniors

### 7. Security Vulnerability Amplification
- 45% of AI code fails security tests [32]
- 2.74x more vulnerabilities than human code [32]
- Java: 72% security failure rate; XSS: 86%; SQL injection: 20% [32]
- Security does NOT improve with model scale -- systemic issue [32]
- 68-73% of Copilot/InCoder samples contained vulnerabilities [17]

### 8. Quality Degradation at Scale ("18-Month Wall") [82]
- Months 1-3: euphoria and accelerated delivery
- Months 4-9: velocity plateau
- Months 10-15: decline (debugging legacy AI code increases)
- Months 16-18: stall (teams can't understand own systems)
- Code churn +84%, refactoring -60%, duplication 8x [23]
- Delivery stability -7.2% per 25% AI adoption [21]

### 9. Benchmark-Reality Gap [78]
- HumanEval: top models 90-96% (data leakage documented)
- SWE-bench Verified: top at 80.9%, average 62.2%
- 20-31 pp drop from HumanEval to HumanEvalNext
- Benchmark scores do not predict production code quality

---

## Cone of Uncertainty: How AI Changes It

### Traditional Cone (McConnell/Construx)
- Initial estimates: 0.25x to 4x actual
- Narrows through decisions and scope reduction

### AI Impact on the Cone

| Effect | Mechanism | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Wider initial cone** (est.) | New uncertainty sources (hallucination, non-determinism, capability boundaries) | Synthesized from [10][79][80] |
| **False narrowing** | AI produces working code quickly, creating illusion of progress | [43] |
| **Late-stage widening** | "18-Month Wall" -- accumulated AI tech debt surfaces | [82] |
| **New dimension** (est.) | "Can the AI actually do this correctly?" -- orthogonal to scope uncertainty | Synthesized from [78][80] |
| **Increased variance** | Non-deterministic output means same task yields different results | [79] |

---

## Quantified Risk Factors for Estimation

| Risk | Probability/Rate | Impact | Mitigation Budget |
|---|---|---|---|
| Package hallucination | 5-20% of recommendations [10] | Supply chain vulnerability | Include dependency verification step |
| Code security failures | 45% of AI code [32] | Security breach | Add security review phase |
| Comprehension debt | Cumulative (progressive) | Team velocity collapse at 12-18 months | Invest in context architecture |
| Context window overflow | Task-dependent | Silent quality degradation | Decompose tasks to 40-60% window [54] |
| Perception gap | 39-44 pp overestimate [3] | Schedule overrun | Use measured velocity, not self-reports |
| Code churn (rework) | +84% vs pre-AI [23] | Rework cycles | Budget 5-15% rework factor |
| Delivery instability | -7.2% per 25% AI adoption [21] | Deployment failures | Maintain testing/QA investment |

---

## Risk Mitigation Strategies

### For Estimation Accuracy
1. **Use measured velocity, never self-reported** -- the 39-44 pp perception gap [3] is real
2. **Apply the Bain Correction** -- multiply coding gains by SDLC coding share (0.25-0.35) [38]
3. **Include rework factor** (5-15%) for AI-introduced bugs and review overhead [27]
4. **Decompose by AI suitability** -- estimate AI-suitable and human-only tasks separately [48]
5. **Budget verification time** explicitly -- 30-50% of implementation time for review/testing

### For Quality Risk
1. **Mandatory code review** for all AI-generated code [28]
2. **Security scanning** -- AI code has 2.74x more vulnerabilities [32]
3. **Context architecture** investment (CLAUDE.md, specs) to prevent comprehension debt [43][77]
4. **Dependency verification** for package hallucination risk [10]
5. **Regular refactoring** to combat the 60% decline in refactoring activity [23]

### For Organizational Risk
1. **Monitor DORA metrics** alongside AI adoption [21]
2. **Watch for the "vacuum hypothesis"** -- time saved absorbed by low-value work [21]
3. **Maintain testing investment** -- don't cut QA because coding is faster
4. **Plan for the "18-Month Wall"** [82] -- schedule periodic codebase health reviews

## Gaps and Limitations

- The "18-Month Wall" pattern [82] is observational/anecdotal, not rigorously studied
- Non-determinism risk is well-understood technically but no estimation framework accounts for it
- The cone of uncertainty analysis for AI is synthesized from multiple sources, not from a single study
- No tool exists to score a task's "AI risk level" prior to estimation
- The Anthropic comprehension study [20] is referenced secondhand; original publication not independently located
